description: Registry/catalog: the workflow designer catalog includes a stable AI grouped record
Registry/catalog: the workflow action registry projection includes the AI inference action and its input/output schema metadata
Designer: dragging or inserting the AI tile creates an `action.call` step rather than a bespoke node type
Designer: AI step hydration preserves grouped action metadata and can recover the AI group from action id when additive metadata is absent
Designer: the AI action prompt field appears in the normal action input section and persists through `inputMapping`
Designer: prompt field supports structured reference mode like other action inputs
Designer: prompt field supports advanced expression mode like other action inputs
Designer: AI step config persists schema mode and inline output schema fields alongside action-call config
Designer: the AI step properties panel renders an output schema authoring section only for the AI inference action
Simple schema builder: author can add and remove top-level output fields
Simple schema builder: primitive field type selection updates the generated schema
Simple schema builder: required flags are reflected in the generated `required` array
Simple schema builder: field descriptions are reflected in the generated JSON Schema
Simple schema builder: nested object fields can be authored and serialized correctly
Simple schema builder: arrays of primitives can be authored and serialized correctly
Simple schema builder: arrays of objects can be authored and serialized correctly
Simple schema builder: root output remains constrained to an object schema
Advanced mode: author can edit raw inline JSON Schema for the AI output
Advanced mode: invalid JSON or invalid schema shape shows inline validation feedback before publish
Hydration: a saved simple-compatible AI schema reopens in simple mode with the correct field tree
Hydration: a saved advanced-only AI schema reopens in advanced mode without lossy conversion
Preview: the properties panel schema reference view shows the resolved AI output schema override instead of the static registry schema
Data context: an upstream AI step contributes its resolved inline output schema to downstream step context
Reference options: downstream source browsing exposes nested AI output fields under `vars.<saveAs>`
Expression context: downstream autocomplete exposes AI output fields under the vars namespace
Designer recompute: changing the AI output schema immediately refreshes downstream reference options for later steps
Publish validation: AI step publish rejects missing inline output schema
Publish validation: AI step publish rejects unsupported simple/advanced schema constructs for v1
Publish validation: valid inline AI schema passes publish-time validation
Type inference: publish-time vars typing uses the resolved AI schema instead of the static action-registry output schema
Persistence: saving and reloading a workflow draft preserves AI prompt, schema mode, and inline output schema
Bundle export/import: exported workflow bundles preserve inline AI schema config and reimport successfully
Bundle dependency validation: inline AI schemas do not create missing schema-ref dependency failures in bundle import
Service: workflow inference service resolves the current provider and model from the existing OpenAI-compatible provider resolver
Service: workflow inference service builds a structured-output request using the step's inline JSON Schema
Service: successful structured AI response is parsed and validated against the inline output schema
Service: malformed model output that fails schema validation is surfaced as a structured workflow-safe error
Service: missing provider configuration fails with an actionable inference error
Service: transient provider rate-limit behavior is retried or normalized consistently before surfacing failure
Runtime integration: the `ai.infer` action handler invokes the workflow inference service and returns structured output on success
Runtime integration: AI action output is validated by the action output schema before persistence
Runtime integration: AI action output saved with `saveAs` is persisted into workflow state and visible to later steps
Guard: AI support does not change downstream typing or publishing behavior for existing non-AI action-call steps
Guard: existing transform steps continue to use their current dynamic output-schema inference path after AI support is added
Guard: existing non-AI workflows still export, import, publish, and run successfully after AI workflow support is added
